reasoning:

The statistical trend analysis from the `fetch_ts_fred` tool establishes a 29% probability that the value will exceed the latest observation (0.5449 on March 14, 2025) by March 23, largely driven by a clear downward linear trend from a recent peak of ~0.70 in January down to the mid-0.50s. To satisfy the instruction that uncertainty should definitively increase with the forecast horizon and approach 0.5 at distant dates, I incrementally raise probabilities toward 50%. This structure is economically sound as term premiums tend to exhibit long-run mean reversion; with 0.54 still being somewhat elevated compared to much of 2024, the probabilities stay below 0.5 as we transition from a statistically bearish short term into maximal uncertainty over the one-year horizon.
